plan.feature: Motor + Fundacao — Correcoes Criticas do Motor de Escalas

phases.name: Foundation: Test Scaffold + Threshold Fix
phases.description: Establish test infrastructure and apply the simplest fix (threshold off-by-one). After this phase we have a working test runner and the B1.4 fix confirmed.

subtasks.description: B4.1 scaffold: Expand test-motor.ts with test runner structure. Add test harness that supports multiple named test cases with PASS/FAIL output. Keep existing 2 tests (basic 4-setor + pinned FOLGA). Add placeholder functions for the 6 remaining tests (lookback, estagiario, R2 descanso, pinned conflict, cobertura impossivel, corte_semanal). Each placeholder returns SKIP for now. Test runner reports: N passed, N failed, N skipped.

details.current_state: test-motor.ts has 97 lines with runMotorTest(db) returning exit code. Has basic 4-setor test + 1 pinned cell test inline.
what_to_do: Refactor runMotorTest into a test runner that calls individual test functions
Each test function returns { name: string, passed: boolean, skipped: boolean, metrics?: any, error?: string }
Keep existing logic for testBasic4Setores() and testPinnedFolgaBasic()
Add 6 placeholder test functions that return { skipped: true }: testLookback, testEstagiarioDomingo, testR2Descanso, testPinnedConflito, testCoberturaImpossivel, testCorteSemanal
Runner prints summary: 'N PASS / N FAIL / N SKIP'
Still exports runMotorTest(db) for Electron integration

verification.expected: No TypeScript errors related to test-motor.ts

subtasks.description: B1.4 threshold: Fix off-by-one in FASE 3 fallback redistribution. Line 270 of gerador.ts: change `tempConsec >= CLT.MAX_DIAS_CONSECUTIVOS` to `tempConsec > CLT.MAX_DIAS_CONSECUTIVOS`. CLT allows 6 consecutive days; the >= forces folga on the 6th day (only allowing 5). FASE 4.5 repair already correctly uses > at line 405.

details.exact_change: Line 270: `if (tempConsec >= CLT.MAX_DIAS_CONSECUTIVOS && weekDays.includes(d))` becomes `if (tempConsec > CLT.MAX_DIAS_CONSECUTIVOS && weekDays.includes(d))`
details.context: This is in the FASE 3 fallback path that fires when initial scoring produces violations. The primary FASE 4.5 repair at line 405 already uses `consec > CLT.MAX_DIAS_CONSECUTIVOS` (correct). Only this FASE 3 fallback has the bug.
details.risk: Minimal — single character change, only affects the redistribution fallback path

phases.name: Core Corrections: corte_semanal + ColabValidacao Expansion + max_minutos + repair pins + metaDiaria
phases.description: Fix the core motor bugs: corte_semanal parameter in getWeeks, expand ColabValidacao interface (bottleneck for 3 corrections), add max_minutos_dia rule, fix repair to respect pinned cells, and unify metaDiariaMin formula. These are tightly coupled changes that must be done together because ColabValidacao expansion affects gerador, validador, and validacao-compartilhada simultaneously.

subtasks.description: B1.1 part 1: Refactor getWeeks() in validacao-compartilhada.ts to accept corte_semanal parameter. Currently line 62-74 splits on 'SEG' hardcoded. New signature: getWeeks(dates: string[], corte_semanal?: string). Default to 'SEG' for backward compatibility. Parse corte_semanal format (e.g., 'QUI_QUA' -> first 3 chars = 'QUI' = start of week). Split weeks when day matches the start day instead of hardcoded 'SEG'.

details.current_code: export function getWeeks(dates: string[]): string[][] { ... diaSemana(d) === 'SEG' ... }
new_code_logic: Add optional param: getWeeks(dates: string[], corte_semanal?: string): string[][]
Parse start day: const startDay = corte_semanal ? corte_semanal.slice(0, 3) as DiaSemana : 'SEG'
Replace hardcoded 'SEG' with startDay in the split condition
Backward compatible: callers without param get SEG behavior
callers_to_update: validacao-compartilhada.ts line 169 (R5 rule) — will be updated in subtask-2-5
gerador.ts line 190 (FASE 3) — will be updated in subtask-2-2

verification.expected: Compiles with no errors (getWeeks default param keeps backward compat)

subtasks.description: B1.1 part 2: Motor (gerador.ts) loads empresa.corte_semanal and passes it to getWeeks() in FASE 3 and to validarRegras() in FASE 6/7. Currently the motor NEVER queries empresa table. Add empresa query in FASE 1 (prep), pass corte_semanal to getWeeks in FASE 3 (line ~190), and propagate to validarRegras call in FASE 6/7.

changes: FASE 1 (prep): Add query: `const empresa = db.prepare('SELECT corte_semanal FROM empresa LIMIT 1').get() as { corte_semanal: string } | undefined`
FASE 3: Pass corteSemanal to getWeeks call (currently `getWeeks(dias)` -> `getWeeks(dias, corteSemanal)`)
FASE 6/7: validarRegras call needs corte_semanal propagated — either pass as extra param or let validarRegras accept it
details.note: validarRegras signature change will be done in subtask-2-5 when we also add corte_semanal param to it

subtasks.description: B1.2 + B2.2 + B2.5 bottleneck: Expand ColabValidacao interface in validacao-compartilhada.ts with max_minutos_dia, dias_trabalho, and trabalha_domingo. This is the critical cross-cutting change that unblocks 3 corrections. Also add new validation rules R_CONTRATO_MAX_DIA and R_ESTAGIARIO_DOMINGO to validarRegras(). Also extract metaDiariaMin as a shared function and add corte_semanal parameter to validarRegras signature.

add_fields: max_minutos_dia: number  // from tipos_contrato (e.g., 570 for CLT 44h, 240 for estagiario)
dias_trabalho: number    // from tipos_contrato (e.g., 6 for CLT 44h, 5 for estagiario)
trabalha_domingo: boolean // from tipos_contrato (false for estagiario)

new_rule_R_CONTRATO_MAX_DIA.logic: For each day: if cel.status === 'TRABALHO' && cel.minutos && cel.minutos > c.max_minutos_dia -> HARD violation

new_rule_R_ESTAGIARIO_DOMINGO.logic: For each day: if !c.trabalha_domingo && diaSemana(d) === 'DOM' && cel.status === 'TRABALHO' -> HARD violation
new_rule_R_ESTAGIARIO_DOMINGO.message_template: ${c.nome}: escalado no domingo mas contrato proibe (trabalha_domingo=false)

metaDiariaMin_shared.logic: export function calcMetaDiariaMin(horas_semanais: number, dias_trabalho: number): number { return Math.round((horas_semanais * 60) / dias_trabalho) }
metaDiariaMin_shared.note: R5 meta semanal at line 167 currently uses /7 which is for weekly tolerance check. The shared function uses /dias_trabalho for daily target (correct for FASE 5 time allocation). R5 should keep using /7 for the weekly tolerance calculation because it checks weekly total vs weekly meta. The shared function is for FASE 5 time slot allocation. Both can coexist — the key is that gerador.ts uses the shared function instead of inline formula.

validarRegras_signature_change.current: validarRegras(colaboradores, resultado, demandas, dias, lookback, tolerancia_min)
validarRegras_signature_change.new: validarRegras(colaboradores, resultado, demandas, dias, lookback, tolerancia_min, corte_semanal?: string)
validarRegras_signature_change.propagate: Pass corte_semanal to getWeeks() call inside R5 (line 169)

verification.expected: TypeScript errors expected in gerador.ts and validador.ts because ColabValidacao expanded but callers not yet updated (these are fixed in subtask-2-4 and phase-3)

subtasks.description: Update gerador.ts to comply with expanded ColabValidacao and new validarRegras signature. The gerador already has ColabComContrato as a superset with all the fields. Ensure the validarRegras call in FASE 6/7 passes corte_semanal. Also update FASE 5 metaDiariaMin to use the shared calcMetaDiariaMin function from validacao-compartilhada.ts.

changes: FASE 5 line 467: Replace `Math.round((c.horas_semanais * 60) / c.dias_trabalho)` with `calcMetaDiariaMin(c.horas_semanais, c.dias_trabalho)`
FASE 6/7: validarRegras call — add corteSemanal as last parameter
Verify ColabComContrato already has max_minutos_dia, dias_trabalho, trabalha_domingo (it does from the JOIN query) — should be a superset of expanded ColabValidacao without changes
details.note: ColabComContrato is defined in gerador.ts and already JOINs tipos_contrato, so it already has all the new fields. TypeScript structural typing means it IS a ColabValidacao automatically if the interface fields match.

verification.expected: gerador.ts compiles cleanly with expanded interface

subtasks.description: B1.3 + B3.1 partial: Fix FASE 4.5 repair to not touch pinned cells. Currently line 406-416 of gerador.ts scores streakDays candidates without filtering out pinned cells. The bestDay chosen could be a pinned TRABALHO cell. Fix: filter pinned cells from the scored array before sorting. If ALL candidates in the streak are pinned, skip the repair entirely (validation will flag the streak later with a HARD violation).

details.current_code_summary: scored = streakDays.map(date => ({ date, score })). scored.sort. bestDay = scored[0].date
fix: After creating scored array (line 413), filter out pinned cells:
If unpinned.length === 0: skip repair for this streak (continue to next iteration). Validation in FASE 6 will flag the consecutive violation.
Else: unpinned.sort((a,b) => b.score - a.score); const bestDay = unpinned[0].date
Rest of the code (lines 417-423) stays the same
details.edge_case: All 7 days pinned as TRABALHO -> repair skips -> FASE 6 validation produces HARD violation R1 -> correct behavior (gestora pinned an impossible streak, she owns it)

verification.expected: Compiles. Pinned cells now excluded from repair candidates.

phases.name: Validator Robustness: Lookback + Timeout + Input Validation
phases.description: Make the validador load lookback from previous OFICIAL escala (B2.1), add worker timeout protection (B2.3), add input validation to motor and IPC (B2.4), and update validador to use expanded ColabValidacao + corte_semanal (B1.1 part 3).

subtasks.description: B2.1 + B1.1 part 3: Update validador.ts to (1) load lookback from previous OFICIAL escala instead of zeroed values, and (2) load empresa.corte_semanal and pass to validarRegras. Currently lines 73-77 create empty lookback (diasConsec:0, domConsec:0). Copy the lookback loading pattern from gerador.ts lines 106-150. Also expand the typed SQL result to include the new ColabValidacao fields (max_minutos_dia, dias_trabalho, trabalha_domingo) which are already in the JOIN query at line 31-46.

logic: Query previous OFICIAL escala for same setor with data_fim < current data_inicio, ORDER BY data_fim DESC LIMIT 1
If found: load last 7 days of alocacoes
Compute diasConsec: count consecutive TRABALHO days at end of previous escala
Compute domConsec: count consecutive Sundays worked at end of previous escala
If NOT found: diasConsec=0, domConsec=0 (current behavior)

empresa_query.add: const empresa = db.prepare('SELECT corte_semanal, tolerancia_semanal_min FROM empresa LIMIT 1').get()
empresa_query.pass: empresa.corte_semanal to validarRegras as last param

colab_typing.add_fields: max_minutos_dia, dias_trabalho, trabalha_domingo to the typed result so ColabValidacao expansion is satisfied

verification.expected: validador.ts compiles with lookback loading + expanded ColabValidacao + corte_semanal

subtasks.description: B2.3: Add 30-second timeout to worker thread in tipc.ts. Both escalas.gerar (lines 592-617) and escalas.ajustar (lines 495-515) spawn workers without timeout. Wrap the worker Promise with Promise.race against a 30s setTimeout. On timeout, call worker.terminate() and reject with humanized error message.

pattern: Create helper function: function withTimeout<T>(workerPromise: Promise<T>, worker: Worker, ms: number = 30000): Promise<T>

Inside: return Promise.race([ workerPromise, new Promise<never>((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => { worker.terminate(); reject(new Error('A geracao demorou mais que o esperado. Tente novamente com menos colaboradores ou um periodo menor.')) }, ms)) ])
Apply to escalas.gerar: `const motor = await withTimeout(workerPromise, worker)`
Apply to escalas.ajustar: `const motor = await withTimeout(workerPromise, worker)`
Refactor: extract the worker Promise creation to a variable before passing to withTimeout

verification.expected: Compiles. Both worker spawns now have timeout.

subtasks.description: B2.4: Add input validation to gerarProposta() and to escalas.ajustar IPC handler. (1) In gerador.ts at top of gerarProposta (line ~72), validate: setor exists in DB, colabs > 0, data_inicio <= data_fim (string comparison works for ISO dates). Throw clear Error messages, not crash on undefined. (2) In tipc.ts escalas.ajustar handler (line 470), add validation: check alocacoes array is not empty, check escala exists (already done), check setor has colabs (new).

checks: const setor = db.prepare('SELECT id FROM setores WHERE id = ? AND ativo = 1').get(setor_id) -- if !setor throw new Error('Setor nao encontrado ou inativo')
if (data_inicio > data_fim) throw new Error('Data inicio deve ser anterior a data fim')
const colabCount = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM colaboradores WHERE setor_id = ? AND ativo = 1').get(setor_id) -- if c === 0 throw new Error('Setor nao tem colaboradores ativos')

tipc_ajustar_validation.location: tipc.ts escalas.ajustar handler (line ~472, after escala check)
checks: if (!input.alocacoes || input.alocacoes.length === 0) throw new Error('Nenhuma alocacao fornecida para ajuste')

phases.name: PinnedCells Complete: Verify All Phases + Worker
phases.description: Verify and fix pinnedCells handling across ALL motor phases (B3.1) and verify worker deserialization (B3.2). Discovery found that most phases already handle pinned cells correctly. FASE 4.5 was fixed in phase-2 (subtask-2-5). This phase is primarily verification with targeted fixes where needed.

subtasks.description: B3.1: Verify and fix pinnedCells handling in FASE 2, FASE 3, FASE 4, and FASE 5 of gerador.ts. Discovery found: FASE 2 OK (lines 160-177 override status correctly), FASE 4 OK (lines 306-387 has isPinned checks), FASE 5 OK (lines 453-464 skip pinned with hours). FASE 3 needs verification: pinned FOLGA cells should count toward folgasNecessarias but not be in the folga pool. Pinned TRABALHO cells should not be forced to FOLGA. Ensure backward compatibility: without pinnedCells param, behavior is identical to current.

fase_2_check.verify: Pinned cells override default status correctly

verify: isPinned filter at line 204-205 prevents pinned cells from entering folga pool -- OK
Pinned FOLGA cells counted via status check at lines 208-211 (restJaGarantido) -- verify this is correct
Pinned TRABALHO cells must NOT be candidates for folga assignment
If a pinned TRABALHO cell exists in a week, it should reduce folgasNecessarias by 0 (it is a work day, not a folga)
If fixes are needed, apply them. If everything is correct per verification, add a comment documenting the verification

fase_4_check.verify: isPinned checks at lines 324, 336, 369. Pinned cells counted for domConsecState/domTotalState at lines 371-374

fase_5_check.verify: Pinned TRABALHO with hours skipped (line 453-464). Pinned TRABALHO without hours falls through to normal allocation. Pinned FOLGA cells skipped in colabsDia filter.

details.backward_compat: Without pinnedCells param: isPinned always returns false, getPinned always returns undefined. All pinned checks become no-ops. Behavior identical to current.

verification.expected: Compiles. All phases handle pinned cells consistently.

subtasks.description: B3.2: Verify worker.ts pinnedCells deserialization compiles after ColabValidacao changes. Discovery confirmed this is ALREADY IMPLEMENTED (worker.ts lines 5-20: WorkerInput has pinnedCellsArr, toPinnedMap converts to Map). Verify TypeScript compiles. If any type adjustments are needed due to ColabValidacao expansion, fix them. This may be ZERO code changes.

details.current_state: worker.ts already has WorkerInput.pinnedCellsArr and toPinnedMap function. PinnedCell type imported from gerador.ts.
details.action: Run npx tsc --noEmit. If worker.ts compiles, no changes needed. If type errors exist from ColabValidacao expansion, fix them. Document verification result either way.

verification.expected: Compiles with zero type errors in worker.ts

phases.name: Test Suite Expansion: All 8+ Test Scenarios
phases.description: Expand test-motor.ts with all 8 test scenarios from PRD RF9 / B4.1. Each test exercises a specific correction. All tests must PASS with 0 HARD violations (except tests that intentionally verify HARD violation detection).

subtasks.description: B4.1 expanded: Implement all 8 test scenarios in test-motor.ts. Replace placeholder SKIP functions with real test implementations. Tests: (1) testBasic4Setores - already exists, verify 0 HARD, pontuacao > 80, cobertura > 90%. (2) testLookback - create OFICIAL escala with 5 consecutive work days at end, generate new escala, verify motor handles cross-escala continuity (0 HARD = motor avoids 7 consecutive). (3) testEstagiarioDomingo - verify estagiario (trabalha_domingo=false) does NOT appear as TRABALHO on any Sunday. (4) testR2Descanso - verify 11h inter-jornada descanso in tight scenario. (5) testPinnedCells - pin 2 cells (1 FOLGA, 1 TRABALHO), verify preservation + 0 HARD. (6) testPinnedConflito - pin 7 consecutive TRABALHO days, verify HARD violation R1. (7) testCoberturaImpossivel - 1 colab, demanda 3, verify low cobertura, SOFT not HARD. (8) testCorteSemanal - change empresa.corte_semanal to QUI_QUA, generate, verify folgas in correct week.

setup: Insert a mock OFICIAL escala for setor 1 with data_fim before test data_inicio
Insert alocacoes: colab 1 has TRABALHO for last 5 days of that escala
Generate new escala for same setor starting from test data_inicio
test_2_lookback.assertion: Motor should avoid creating 7 consecutive (5 from lookback + 2 from new). 0 HARD violations.
test_2_lookback.cleanup: Delete mock OFICIAL escala after test

test_3_estagiario.setup: Identify estagiario colabs (via tipos_contrato WHERE trabalha_domingo = 0)
test_3_estagiario.assertion: In generated escala, no estagiario has TRABALHO on any DOM day

test_4_r2_descanso.setup: Use existing seed data (already has varied shifts)
test_4_r2_descanso.assertion: No colab has < 11h (660min) between end of day N and start of day N+1. Check via alocacoes hora_fim and hora_inicio of consecutive work days.
test_4_r2_descanso.note: This validates FASE 5 time allocation respects R2

test_5_pinned_cells.setup: Pin 2 cells: colab 1 on day 5 = FOLGA, colab 2 on day 10 = TRABALHO
test_5_pinned_cells.assertion: Both cells preserved in output. 0 HARD violations.

test_6_pinned_conflito.setup: Pin 7 consecutive TRABALHO days for colab 1 (days 1-7 of period)
test_6_pinned_conflito.assertion: HARD violation R1 (MAX_DIAS_CONSECUTIVOS) exists for colab 1. This is correct — gestora pinned an impossible streak.

test_7_cobertura_impossivel.setup: Temporarily modify demandas for a setor to require 3 people, but only have 1 colab (or use a setor with naturally impossible coverage)
test_7_cobertura_impossivel.assertion: Motor generates without crash. Cobertura < 100%. SOFT violation (not HARD) for coverage.

test_8_corte_semanal.assertion: Weeks are split QUI-QUA, not SEG-DOM. Folgas distributed within correct weekly boundaries.
